original_abstract: International Higher Education (IHE) delivers expert, nuanced commentary and rigorous analysis on the evolving trends and critical challenges facing global higher education. By examining shifting funding models—from the public-good traditions of Western Europe to the market-driven privatized systems of the US and Australia—IHE explores how institutions and stakeholders navigate a complex global landscape. This publication provides essential insights into the multifaceted dimensions of internationalization, including student mobility, the expansion of branch campuses, and the persistent inequalities that shape the relationship between emerging academic systems and established global superpowers.
humanized_abstract: International Higher Education (IHE) provides thought leadership and a nuanced and analytically rich look at developments and issues of major importance in the field of global higher education. IHE examines the change in funding models from the public good tradition of Western Europe to the market-oriented privatized system of the US and Australia and the ways in which institutions and other actors cope with this new environment. IHE delves into the multiple facets of internationalization including student mobility, the proliferation of branch campuses and other forms of transnational provision, and the persistent inequalities at the heart of the relationship between new and developing systems of higher education and the established global higher education power structure.
